lines.en_lit: gains forgiveness only the sin of impurity, which was exempted from its general in the public. a difficulty to Rav Sheshet, is a dispute between tanna’im, as it was taught The frontplate effects acceptance whether it is on forehead or whether it is not on forehead the statement of Rabbi Shimon.

Rabbi Yehuda says: As long as it is on his forehead it effects acceptance; it is no longer on his forehead it does not effect acceptance. Rabbi Shimon said to The High Priest on Yom Kippur prove as no longer on his forehead, and effects acceptance.
Rabbi Yehuda said to him: Leave the High Priest on Yom Kippur, as impurity is permitted in the public. by inference Rabbi Shimon holds that impurity is overridden in the public,
Abaye said: where the frontplate broke, everyone, agrees that no effects acceptance. When they disagree is hanging on a peg. Rabbi Yehuda holds on the forehead and shall gain forgiveness

And Rabbi Shimon holds be always be accepted before the Lord.” what is always If we say always on forehead, do you find Doesn’t he need to enter the bathroom, doesn’t he need to sleep, Rather, always effects acceptance,

And to Rabbi Yehuda as well, isn’t it written: “Always”? That “Always,” that he should not be distracted from it. in accordance with of Rabba bar Rav Huna, as Rabba bar Rav Huna said: A person must touch phylacteries his each and every hour, an a fortiori from the frontplate:
